question: Why did the Production negative test report 9 errors when the active root contains 13 blocking domain decisions across 9 artifacts?
answer: One Production-readiness error represents one blocking artifact identity (DocumentType+Key+Version), not one field-level decision. errorCount(9) matches blockingArtifactCount(9) by construction, not blockingDecisionCount(13). The relationship was already correct; the defect was that the 13 nested decisions were only reachable via a concatenated free-text message string, not structured data. That defect is now fixed.

steps: for each of the 9 tuples: PilotDomainContentAudit.BlockingEntriesFor(documentType,key,version) -> 1..2 DomainContentDecision rows each (13 total)
one ContentDecisionGuardError emitted per artifact tuple with >=1 blocking entry, carrying ALL of that artifact's blocking decisions structured (post-fix) or concatenated into Message (pre-fix)
CatalogPublisher throws CatalogValidationException carrying both the legacy ValidationResult (9 Issues) and (post-fix) the structured ContentDecisionGuardResult

CLI ReleaseCommands.Publish/BuildRelease catch block prints Issues (9) and, post-fix, the structured Data object (BlockingArtifactCount=9, BlockingDecisionCount=13, Errors[9] each with nested BlockingDecisions)
codePath.cardinalityRule: one ValidationIssue / ContentDecisionGuardError per distinct blocking artifact identity

requiredTrace.informationRetained: Message text + hardcoded JsonPath='$' only; Classification/Reason/EntryId dropped
full: EntryId, FieldPath, Classification, Reason nested per error, plus BlockingArtifactCount/BlockingDecisionCount aggregate
identical to previous stage; no re-parsing
full; never derived from ValidationIssue list

totals.note: True after this task's fix. Before the fix, all 13 were present only as substrings of 9 free-text Message strings (technically present, not structurally parseable).

contractOptions.description: Artifact-grouped top-level errors with complete nested field-level decisions
reasonsChosen: avoids noisy duplicate top-level errors for artifacts with multiple blocking fields
preserves artifact context per error without client-side re-grouping
consistent with docs/README.md's decision-vs-artifact-level metrics separation
fully backward compatible with existing ValidationResult-based tests and callers
matches the repository's pre-existing architecture (grouping key was already artifact-level; only nested detail was missing)

defectFound.classification: groups by artifact but loses decision detail
defectFound.description: ValidationIssue (the return type of PublishReadinessValidator.ValidateContentDecisions) is a flat record {Code,Severity,Message,JsonPath} with no field for a nested decision list. All 13 blocking decisions were concatenated into free-text Message strings; JsonPath was hardcoded to '$'. Classification, Reason, and audit-entry ID were entirely absent from the structured result.
defectFound.violatesRequirement: Do not encode all nested decision data only inside one concatenated message string.

codeChanges: src/PlanCatalog.Core/Validation/PublishReadinessValidator.cs - added ValidateContentDecisionsDetailed(...); ValidateContentDecisions(...) now delegates to it via ToValidationResult() (same signature/return type, same issue count)

src/PlanCatalog.Core/Validation/CatalogValidationException.cs - added optional nullable ContentDecisionGuardResult? ContentDecisionDetail property/constructor param (default null, fully backward compatible)
src/PlanCatalog.Infrastructure/Publishing/CatalogPublisher.cs - content-decision-guard stage now calls ValidateContentDecisionsDetailed and attaches the structured result to the thrown exception
src/PlanCatalog.Cli/Commands/ReleaseCommands.cs - BuildRelease/Publish catch blocks pass ex.ContentDecisionDetail as CliOutput.Report's data parameter instead of null
src/PlanCatalog.Cli/Commands/CliOutput.cs - NO CHANGE REQUIRED (already serializes any non-null data object as structured JSON in both text and --json modes)

releaseHandling.reason: Only validator diagnostics, error payload/model, CLI wiring, tests, and audit/governance documentation changed.

commandsRun: publish --channel Production --allow-unconfirmed-content (text and --json) against real retirement ledger
verify-release for all 7 releases (1.0.0 .. 0.6.0-pilot) - all PASSED
preExistingGranularityNote: Two of the 13 DomainContentDecision audit rows (AUD-044, GOAL_PACE_TEN_K's entry AUD-249) each bundle multiple field paths into a single row's JsonPath string. This predates this task, is not a defect this task was asked to fix (domain-content audit granularity, not error-contract structure), and is preserved 1:1 (not modified) by this task's fix.
